title: Understand: The reasons for the Revolution
description: Check your understanding of the reasons for the American Revolution in this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Principles of the American Revolution: The Ideas and Events That Led to Change
Understand: The reasons for the Revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
**In response to the taxation policies of Great Britain, which founder published a series of essays titled *Letters from a Farmer in Pennsylvania to the Inhabitants of the British Colonies* in 1768?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Benjamin Franklin
Thomas Paine
John Dickinson
Robert Morris

assessment_items.question_content:
**What influential work did Thomas Paine publish in 1776?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: *Common Sense*
The Declaration of Independence
*Thoughts on Government*
The Olive Branch Petition

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Thomas Paine, the idea that monarchs get their authority directly from God, known as the divine right of kings, was \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: true only in Europe, not America
false, unless the king proved to be a good king
true, but the American colonists should ignore it
false in all circumstances

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which word best describes the colonists’ view of or attitude toward King George III In 1760?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Critical
Favorable
Hostile
Uninterested

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these was a main cause of the American Revolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A lack of religious freedom in the colonies
A lack of representation in government
A desire to create an American monarchy
A desire to reform the British government

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the purpose of Thomas Paine’s *Common Sense* pamphlet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To argue that the colonies should remain under British rule
To encourage an alliance with the French against the British government
To legally declare independence from the British government
To encourage American colonies to seek independence

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the purpose of the Stamp Act?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To raise funds from the colonies for the British government
To punish the colonies for their participation in the Boston Tea Party
To give the colonies more representation in the British government
To ban the purchase and sale of goods from other countries in the colonies

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the primary message of the Massachusetts Circular Letter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The British Parliament is the supreme legislative power, and the colonies have no right to oppose any laws it passes.
Colonies have a right to revolt against an unjust government, so the Americans should declare their independence.
The British government cannot tax the colonies if there is no colonial representation in Parliament.
Monarchy is a tyrannical form of government, while republicanism protects individual liberties from government overreach.

assessment_items.question_content:
John Dickinson’s *Letters from a Farmer in Pennsylvania* was important in encouraging colonial independence. 

**What was one of the most significant arguments Dickinson made?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is no difference between external and internal taxation.
There are no representative governments in the colonies.
There is no choice other than revolution against the monarchy.
The only true form of government is a monarchy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which laws passed by the British led the American colonists to resist British rule? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Federal Judiciary Act
The Stamp Act
The Townshend Acts
The Coercive Acts
The Reconstruction Acts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the British response to the Boston Tea Party?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To repeal all taxes on the colonies and appoint a colonial representative in Parliament
To officially declare war on the American colonies
To remove all British troops from the Massachusetts colony
To pass more restrictive policies on the Massachusetts colony

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the initial colonial response to the Coercive Acts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To submit a list of grievances against the British government and declare independence
To protest against the new taxes on tea by throwing imported tea into Boston Harbor
To assemble the First Continental Congress and pass a resolution saying Parliament had no right to tax the colonies
To consent to the Coercive Acts and allow Parliament to punish the Massachusetts colony
